prompt:
Escalation from the Kettle Ridge aggregates yard — the nightly weighbridge export report is wrong in three separate ways and month-end billing is frozen until it's right. I spent the afternoon with a stack of paper scale tickets and a calculator, so I can give you exact numbers; what I can't give you is the why. This crate is the whole pipeline.

1) Loads over the legacy outbound lane come out absurdly light. Trailer 24,800 lb on the paper ticket from the legacy head: by hand that's 24,800 x 0.45359237 = 11,249.09 kg, so the shift line should read 11.25 t. The report credits 5.11 t. We ran the SAME trailer over the new inbound lane, its head punched the same 24,800 lb, and the report shows 11.25 t exactly right. Metric loads on the legacy lane are fine too — it's specifically legacy-lane pound tickets that shrink, and the shrink factor looks suspiciously like the lb->kg factor itself.

2) Trucks weighed at shift change go to the wrong shift. Day shift runs 06:00-14:00, swing runs from 14:00. A 12,405 kg load punched at exactly 14:00:00 shows up in the 06:00 shift's totals, and the swing shift starts the afternoon short. The scale house swears their export is sorted and the punch minute on the ticket is right — I checked, it is (minute 840). Anything punched 14:01 or later lands correctly.

3) Even on clean, new-lane-only, all-kilogram days the shift totals read a hundredth of a tonne heavy versus hand math on about half the lines — and it is never light, always heavy. Example from Tuesday: two gravel tickets, 4,101 kg + 8,302 kg = 12,403 kg = 12.403 t. Site rule is nearest 0.01 t with halves going up, so that's 12.40 t. The report prints 12.41 t. Totals that hand-round to an exact hundredth (like 12,400 kg) match perfectly, which is why spot checks kept passing.

`cargo test --offline` reproduces all three failures exactly as described (plus the healthy paths that must keep working). Please root-cause and fix the library under src/ — all three could be one bug or three, I genuinely don't know. Do not touch tests/, Cargo.toml, or .gitignore, no public API changes, stdlib only. Done when the whole suite is green.
